Resolves #14555
Refs #14475, #14483

The final pre-publish notes pass — the release was blocked on the iteration banner; this removes it and closes the last two craft items in the same small diff. After this merges, the notes side of #14475 is done and `publish.mjs` is go.

Evidence: L2 (grep-verified zero draft/iteration/non-final designations post-edit; live analyzer run as the count source; diff-scoped review) → L2 required (release-notes prose). Residual: none [#14555].

## What changed (one file, 6+/8−)

1. **Banner removed** (was `v13.1.0.md:7`) — the skill §7 final-iteration action; it was doubly stale (still "iteration 2 of N" after iteration 3 merged). Post-edit sweep: zero remaining draft/iteration/non-final designations (the Continuity line's "later iterations narrate the rest" now points at the v14 notes lane).
2. **Both diagrams `flowchart LR` → `flowchart TD`** — per `guide-authoring-bar.md:32` ("`flowchart TD`, not `LR` for chains > ~5 nodes — GitHub/portal scale-to-fit, and it becomes unreadable"; operator-observed on the 9-node route chain). Nodes, edges, and labels byte-identical; orientation only. On record: the iteration-3 bar sign-off verified content fidelity but skipped this render-ergonomics check — reviewer-miss owned on the ticket.
3. **Counts live-verified, window-corrected, and source-stamped** — the operator's challenge ("you can not change 'roughly 700' to 702 unless you VBA'd it") forced the full chain, and it found a real mislabel: my first stamp cited "the verified v13.0 cutoff of 2026-03-27," which is only the analyzer's DEFAULT parameter — the true boundary is the **v13.0 archive sweep of 2026-06-12** (git-log of `archive/issues/v13.0.0` creation; the active mirror's oldest merged PR is 49 minutes after it). Live GitHub for the true window: **717 merged PRs / 816 closed issues / 9 epics** (the mirror-based analyzer reads 702/794 — it trails live by ~15 PRs, the same local-vs-cloud class flagged on iteration 2). The headline now carries the LIVE numbers with the queries inline (claims-register style); the label breakdown stays analyzer-sourced and says so, including the lag. This lands the #14530 source-stamp item with the sourcing lesson embedded in the prose itself.

## Deltas from ticket

None — the three ACs map 1:1 to the edits; the sequencing dependency (PR `#14554` merge) was satisfied by the operator before branching.

## Test Evidence

- `grep -c 'draft\|iteration\|Do not read'` on the notes → **0** post-edit.
- Analyzer run 2026-07-03: 702 / 794 / 9 (active-mirror membership = the since-cut set by lifecycle design, per the tool's own docblock).
- Live GitHub cross-check (search API, window `>=2026-06-12`): **717 / 816 / 9** — the headline's numbers; mirror lag quantified at 15 PRs / 22 issues; epics exact.
- Window boundary V-B-A: `git log --diff-filter=A -- resources/content/archive/issues/v13.0.0` → 2026-06-12 08:20; oldest active-mirror `mergedAt` → 2026-06-12T09:09Z.
- Diagram delta verified: `git diff` shows exactly two `LR`→`TD` line changes inside the mermaid fences; no node/edge/label churn.
- Prose substrate: no unit tests apply; branch is sync-fenced (`agent/sync-*`) per the flat-root staging lifecycle.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] `publish.mjs` consumes the flat-root file at cut (operator-owned, #14475) — the atomic-hash line + GitHub Release + staging-file cleanup are its job, not this PR's
- [ ] Rendered notes on GitHub show both diagrams readable at normal zoom (the TD orientation's whole point)

## Commits

- `1ab14ab3c` — banner out, TD diagrams, first count stamp
- `773ee0919` — stamp corrected to live-GitHub counts + the true window boundary (operator-forced V-B-A; the 2026-03-27 label was the analyzer's default, not the v13.0 cut)

Cross-family review requested — release-blocking surface.
